libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set JGroups tests and native runtime args to bind locally: -Djgroups.bind_addr=127.0.0.1 and -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true. Prefer SHARED_LOOPBACK or similarly local in-process protocol stacks for JChannel tests; do not rely on UDP multicast, external hosts, or a real database for JDBC_PING unless separate setup is intentionally added. Use jgroups.use.jdk_logger=true or an explicit jgroups.log_class custom logger when a test is not intentionally covering logger backend selection.

deterministic_setup.reason: The resolved JGroups POM declares slf4j-api as optional, and LogFactory detects org.slf4j.Logger reflectively; a plain org.jgroups:jgroups dependency will not bring it in.
SslContextFactory reflectively probes org.wildfly.openssl.OpenSSLProvider and SSL, and the existing JGroups reachability metadata/test scaffold covers this optional path.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8866 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.jgroups:jgroups:5.2.2.Final; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: wildfly-openssl can change the selected SSL provider; tests should allow either the JDK provider path or the OpenSSL-detected path where appropriate.
JDBC_PING's command-line/default examples assume an HSQLDB server, but meaningful coverage can be achieved with a stub Driver/DataSource, so no Docker database is justified up front.
The final decision remains subject to local Gradle and Native Image verification for the 5.2.2.Final run failure.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: JGroups core can be tested without Docker, but stable meaningful coverage needs loopback/IPv4 system properties, and useful native metadata coverage reaches optional logging/OpenSSL integrations that are not provided by the normal transitive artifact.
